title: poa aucklandica subsp. aucklandica
intro: Poa aucklandica subsp. aucklandica, also known as Auckland grass, is a rare, perennial grass species endemic to the Auckland Islands in New Zealand. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as phenolic acids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Auckland grass include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to exhibit antioxidant and cytotoxic effects. Auckland grass is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are then used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
